questions.question: Who is eligible to migrate their existing data to the EU region?

options.text: All Enterprise Plan customers, regardless of account team assistance
options.explanation: Only Enterprise Plan customers who are sales-assisted (working with an account team) can migrate existing data to the EU region.

options.text: Standard Team and Pro Plan customers
options.explanation: Data residency is an Enterprise Plan feature and is not available for Standard or Pro plans.
This is correct. Sales-assisted Enterprise customers can contact their account team to set up the migration.

options.text: Free Plan users with a paid add-on
options.explanation: Data residency is a feature reserved for Enterprise Plan customers.

questions.question: Which category of data is NOT stored at rest in the customer's selected data region?

options.explanation: User account information is listed under 'Data stored outside the data region' as it may be processed in international locations.
Page content is one of the categories explicitly listed as being stored at rest in the customer’s selected region.
The search index is listed as data stored at rest in the customer's selected region.
Files such as images and documents uploaded to Notion are stored at rest in the customer's selected region.

questions.question: How does Notion handle backups for data stored in the EU data region?

options.text: Backups are stored in the US-East-2 region
options.explanation: Backups are stored within the same region as production, which would be the EU region (EU-West-1) if the customer chose the EU data region.

options.text: Backups are stored in a decentralized public ledger
options.explanation: Notion uses AWS databases with automated backups stored in the same region as production, not a decentralized ledger.

options.text: Backups are stored within the same region as production
options.explanation: This is correct. Notion stores backups within the same region as the production data to ensure reliability and compliance.
Backups are encrypted and monitored daily; they are not deleted after 30 days. US data is deleted after migration, but backups persist.

questions.question: When Customer Data is processed outside of the customer's selected data region, which of the following scenarios describes this?

options.text: When a user creates a new page
options.explanation: Creating a new page stores the data in the customer's selected region.

options.text: When duplicating content from another workspace or moving content from a US workspace to an EU workspace
options.explanation: This is correct. Duplicating content or moving synced blocks from a US workspace involves processing data outside the EU.

options.text: When searching for keywords within a database
options.explanation: Search index data is stored in the customer's selected region.

options.text: When viewing a public share link
options.explanation: Viewing public share links generally processes data within the selected region.

questions.question: Which products are explicitly excluded from the data residency offering?

options.explanation: Notion Databases and Notion AI are covered by data residency; Notion Calendar is not.
This is correct. Notion Calendar, Notion Mail, and any Beta Services are not covered by data residency.
Existing integrations remain enabled. However, data processed by these integrations is outside the scope, but the calendar/mail apps themselves are the specific exclusion.
Files uploaded to Notion are explicitly included in the data residency categories.

questions.question: What is the cost of enabling data residency for eligible Enterprise Plan customers?

options.text: It requires a monthly subscription fee
options.explanation: Data residency is available free of charge to customers on the Enterprise Plan.
This is correct. The feature is free for Enterprise Plan customers.

options.text: It costs a one-time setup fee
options.explanation: There is no setup fee mentioned; it is simply enabled at no extra cost.

options.text: It is only free for sales-assisted customers
options.explanation: The cost is free for all Enterprise Plan customers, regardless of whether they are sales-assisted or not.

questions.question: What happens to Customer Data stored in the United States after a workspace is successfully migrated to the EU data region?

options.text: It is moved to the US backup region
options.explanation: After migration, the US data is deleted after 30 days, it is not moved to a backup region.
There is a grace period of 30 days after the workspace is migrated before the US data is deleted.
This is correct. The categories of Customer Data described will be deleted in the US after 30 days following the migration.

options.text: It remains indefinitely in the US
options.explanation: No, the data is removed from the US after the 30-day period post-migration.

questions.question: How does Notion AI handle data residency for Enterprise Plan customers?

options.text: The data is stored in the US regardless of the customer's region setting
options.explanation: The Customer Data described is stored in the EU, but the LLM providers have zero data retention.

options.text: The data is stored in the EU, but LLM providers have zero data retention
options.explanation: This is correct. The Customer Data is stored in the EU, but the LLM providers used for Notion AI utilize zero data retention for Enterprise Plan workspaces.

options.text: Notion AI does not process any Customer Data
options.explanation: Notion AI does process Customer Data, and the specific categories are stored in the customer's region.

options.text: The data is processed by LLM providers who retain data for 30 days
options.explanation: LLM providers have zero data retention for Enterprise Plan workspaces, not a 30-day retention.

questions.question: Where are backups for data stored in the US data region located?

options.explanation: This is correct. The table shows that US-West-2 (Oregon) has a backup region of US-East-2 (Ohio).
US-East-2 (Ohio) is the backup region for US-West-2, not the primary storage region itself.
The EU region has its own backup region in Europe (Ireland), distinct from US backups.
AWS provides durable infrastructure, but backups are stored within the same region as production.

questions.question: By default, where is a new workspace hosted?

options.explanation: This is correct. By default, data will continue to reside in the United States.
The EU is the default for migration, not for new workspaces.

options.text_explanation: The article mentions US and EU regions.

options.text: The user can choose any region upon creation
options.explanation: The article states data 'will continue to reside' in the US, implying the default is US.

questions.question: What should a sales-assisted Enterprise Plan customer do to set up a data residency migration?

options.explanation: While non-sales-assisted customers contact email, sales-assisted customers must contact their account team.
This is correct. Sales-assisted Enterprise customers must contact their account team to set up the migration.

options.text: Change their billing address in settings
options.explanation: Migrating data residency is a separate process from billing settings.
Migration is not automated; it requires contact with the account team.

questions.question: Which of the following is considered outside the scope of Notion’s data residency offering?

options.explanation: Page content is explicitly covered by data residency.

options.text: Data hosted or processed by integrations and non-Notion Services
options.explanation: This is correct. Data processed by integrations and non-Notion Services is outside the scope of the data residency offering.
The search index is covered by data residency.

options.text: Usage information and data used to measure seat count
options.explanation: Usage information is listed as data stored outside the data region, but integrations processing data are specifically mentioned as outside the scope of the *offering*.

questions.question: How does data residency affect existing integrations on a workspace?

options.explanation: Integrations are not removed immediately; they remain enabled.
Integrations are not automatically disabled upon enabling data residency.
This is correct. Previously enabled apps remain enabled.

options.text: They are migrated to the EU region
options.explanation: The integrations themselves remain, but the data they process is outside the scope of Notion's data residency offering.
